data.description:
## Why

New local sessions/worktrees on this machine currently write ticket events **unsigned** —
there is no persistent `identity.signing_key`, no `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`, and no
`.rebar/current_identity` pointer. Only ~4 one-off validation-demo signed events exist
store-wide. Until every local session signs in steady state, flipping
`identity.require_authenticated` would reject every local write.

## What

Provision a **persistent** signing key so every new shell/session/worktree on this machine
signs with no per-session setup:

- Configure the private key via a per-machine, git-ignored `[identity] signing_key` (or
  export `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` from the shell profile).
- Confirm the writer's `git config user.email` (`joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`) resolves to an
  identity whose registered **public** key matches the configured private key.

## Scope

This machine's local clones only. GitHub Actions and Gerrit-side signing are separate
stories. No enforcement flip here (that is the atomic-flip story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A persistent signing key is configured such that a **fresh** shell/session/worktree
      signs writes with no per-session setup.
- [ ] In a fresh worktree, `rebar comment <t> "x"` then `rebar show <t>` shows
      `authorship: {signed: >=1}`, and `rebar verify-authorship` reports it `verified`.
- [ ] The identity's registered public key matches the configured private key, and
      `git config user.email` matches the identity email.
- [ ] [operator-attested] the private key file is `0600`, lives outside the repo, and the
      setup is captured in the local runbook (record the key path + identity id).
data.title: Provision persistent local write-signing on this machine (all worktrees/sessions)
